expectations.text: manifest.json present with valid reverse-DNS UUID and action
Auth uses PKCE flow OR PAT stored securely in global settings
expectations.evidence: Uses Authorization Code flow with client_secret — not PKCE, not PAT

expectations.text: Token stored in streamDeck.settings.setGlobalSettings (NOT action settings)
expectations.evidence: accessToken in global settings BUT client_secret saved to per-action setSettings()

expectations.text: GitHub API called with Authorization header
Button title updated with PR count using setTitle()
streamDeck.system.openUrl() used to open browser to GitHub
Property inspector HTML file exists
plugin.ts registers action and calls streamDeck.connect()
rollup.config.mjs and package.json present

notes: Security issue: client_secret stored in action settings (plain text, exported with profiles)
Used fixed port 57321 instead of dynamic port — non-compliant for providers requiring exact URI match

OBS and system-stats assertions are non-discriminating — both configs scored 100%. Future evals should test what the skill uniquely teaches: dynamic OAuth port vs fixed port, no client_secret in action settings, event-driven vs polling architecture choice.
GitHub is the only discriminating eval: with_skill avoided client_secret in action settings; without_skill stored it there (security vulnerability).
with_skill ran 59 seconds faster on average and used 4,420 fewer tokens — reference files gave the agent a direct path.

with_skill GitHub 'failure' on assertion 5 (setTitle) is a false negative — agent used setImage with dynamic SVG per skill guidance on dynamic images. Assertion should be broadened to accept either approach.
Key skill gap identified: no guidance on which OAuth providers support PKCE vs require client_secret. GitHub does not support PKCE — skill should recommend PAT for GitHub integrations.
